PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-28-2016 Continued To Apr-14-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Brown for and with (petitioner) Reynaldo Morales. Vincent Russo (via Court Call) for Melinda Bennett who is present in Court. Todd Bennett (spouse of Melinda) also present.

Mr. Brown reported that they have filed an opposition to Ms. Bennett's petition and that they request that Reynaldo be appointed as administrator. Mr. Russo stated that they want Ms. Bennett appointed and the 2013 will probated, that Mr. Bennett received notice and is present in Court, that the proof of publication was filed yesterday, that they need a capacity declaration as to who should be appointed and that they request an evidentiary hearing in 90 days and will have 5 witnesses plus an expert and expect to be done in 2 half days. Mr. Brown stated that he is prepared to submit on the papers now. Mr. Russo stated that he is also prepared to submit on the pleadings now, that they do not require an evidentiary hearing as it would not change the declarations already submitted. Mr. Brown stated that Ms. Bennett submitted an invalid will (2013 will), that an original of the 1992 will was not lodged, asks that Mr. Morales be appointed as administrator, that it does not matter if they operate under the 1992 will and that Ms. Bennett is not trustworthy to serve. Mr. Russo stated that the 1992 was lodged with the Court on December 1, 2016 with the 2013 will, that the 2013 will was witnessed by two of the children, that the residue clause states that all 3 of the children receive the residue, that the 2013 will is valid, that they submitted the 1992 will as well for the Court to decide, that even if the property passes intestate the outcome is the same, that there is only one asset which will be split 50/50 between the two and that Reynaldo has health issues, his capacity is in question and he is not fit to be administrator. The Court stated that this will need more argument and will require more evidence and recessed it until after the remaining hearings. The Court recalled the matter and confirmed with Mr. Russo that the Court has all of the information and that he is not requesting an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Russo stated that is correct assuming that the Court has read the original objections. Mr. Brown confirmed as well. The Court confirmed that there will be no additional filings or submissions. Mr. Brown gave the Court background information, stated that the 2013 will disinherits Mr. Morales and leaves everything to Ms. Bennett, that the Court can question Mr. Morales regarding his competency, that the declaration of Ms. Bennett states that Mr. Morales is entitled to 1/3 instead of 1/2 and asks that the 2013 will not be admitted. Mr. Russo stated that the 2013 will was witnessed by two of the children, that it names all 3 of the children as beneficiaries, that one child is now deceased with no issue, that there is some ambiguity in the 2013 will but the end result is the same no matter who is appointed, that there is one piece of property to be split equally, that in regards to Mr. Morales capacity he suffers from health and memory issues and cited 8402. Mr. Brown stated that opposing counsel is overlooking paragraph 3 in the 2013 will which leaves all property to Ms. Bennett, that there are text messages stating that Mr. Morales is to only receive 1/3 share and that none of Mr. Morales' health issues render him incompetent. The Court stated that it will look at everything, that it will set an evidentiary hearing date of April 14, 2017 and the Petition for Letters of Administration and the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary were continued to that date. Mr. Bennett confirmed that he waives notice. Mr. Brown stated that there is no issue with notice and asked about appointing co-special administrators. The Court stated that it would need an ex parte petition and hearing and that it should be a neutral party.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-21-2016 Continued To Dec-28-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David Brown for and with Reynaldo Morales. Vincent Russo (via court call) for (objector) Melinda Bennett.

Mr. Russo reported that they have had difficulty with e-filing, that they have two wills, one of which was drafted prior to the second marriage and that they are mailing them to the Court. Mr. Brown stated that this is a delay, that it should have been filed by now and asked if his client could be appointed as special administrator. The Court stated that it would not appoint a special administrator without a petition for such. Mr. Russo confirmed that he will need to file his petition in the existing case number. The Petition for letters of administration was continued to December 28, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Ideta. (Not Reported)
